faqs.question: What pantry pests are most common in Edgewater, NJ?
faqs.answer: Edgewater residents most commonly encounter Indian meal moths, cigarette beetles, grain weevils, and flour beetles. These pests infest dry goods like cereals, flour, rice, pasta, nuts, and pet food. High-rise buildings along the waterfront can experience faster spread between units, making prompt professional treatment essential.

This includes a thorough inspection, treatment of all infested zones, and follow-up recommendations to prevent re-infestation.

faqs.question: Will I need to throw away all my pantry food?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily all of it. Our technician will inspect your pantry items and identify which products are infested or at risk. Typically, opened packages of dry goods should be discarded, while sealed, unaffected items can often be saved. We'll provide clear guidance on what to keep and what to discard, plus tips on proper storage to prevent future infestations.
